skill.name: reply
skill.description: Review p11 document comments and replies, then post useful replies to visible threads that ask questions, are unclear, are indecisive, or need input. Use when Codex should answer reviewers on a p11 document.
skill.summary: Review p11 document comments and post replies to threads that need input or clarification.
skill.author: rarexlabs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 3 weak cryptographic algorithm matches and 21 shell backtick execution patterns. All crypto matches are false positives: a hex UI color code in openai.yaml and the project name 'p11' in SKILL.md frontmatter. The external commands are markdown-documented CLI commands for the p11 tool, used legitimately for code review operations. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or obfuscation found. Risk level assessed as low.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match - Hex Color Code
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged '#2563EB' at agents/openai.yaml:3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm. This is a hex color code used for the UI brand_color field. It has no cryptographic function and is a false positive.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match - Project Name
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 'p11' in SKILL.md frontmatter as a weak cryptographic algorithm reference. 'p11' is the name of the document review CLI tool used by this skill, not a PKCS#11 cryptographic reference. This is a false positive.

low_findings.title: External Commands in CLI Documentation
low_findings.description: The static analyzer detected 21 instances of backtick-enclosed text in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell execution. All instances are markdown code formatting used to document legitimate p11 CLI commands (p11 comments, p11 reply, p11 history). No command injection, obfuscation, or arbitrary shell execution is present.

content.user_title: Reply to p11 Document Review Comments
content.value_statement: Reviewing code review comments and drafting replies takes time away from coding. This skill inspects p11 document threads and posts helpful replies where input or clarification is needed.
actual_capabilities: Fetch all visible comments and replies from a p11 document using the p11 CLI
Analyze each visible thread to determine if a reply is needed based on thread content
Post replies to threads that ask questions, request clarification, or need input
Skip threads with clear settled decisions that should be handled by p11:revise
Use --json format to examine structured comment fields when useful
Summarize visible threads that need no reply and identify settled decisions
limitations: Cannot reply to hidden or resolved comments that reviewers cannot see
Cannot modify document content or apply suggested changes
Cannot access p11 documents not mentioned in the current conversation
Reply quality depends on the clarity of the original comment content

use_cases.title: Code Review Comment Triage
use_cases.description: Automatically review and respond to questions and unclear comments on p11 code review documents.

use_cases.title: Document Review Clarification
use_cases.description: Post clarifying questions on document review threads that lack clear decisions or need additional input.

use_cases.title: Review Response Delegation
use_cases.description: Let Codex handle routine reply drafting while you focus on substantive code changes and revisions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Comment Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the p11 document [target]. Check all visible comment threads and post replies where clarification is needed.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time use to review comments on a single document

prompt_templates.title: Thread-Specific Reply
prompt_templates.prompt: Look at the p11 document [target]. Review all visible threads and reply to any that ask questions or need input. Summarize threads that have clear decisions for p11:revise.
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted review when you need replies on specific open threads

prompt_templates.title: Review Triage with Named Replies
prompt_templates.prompt: Review comments on p11 document [target]. For each visible thread, decide if a reply is needed. Post concise replies with the name Codex. If no threads need replies, summarize clear decisions for p11:revise.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full triage workflow with consistent reply naming

prompt_templates.title: Bulk Reply with Structured Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Process all visible threads on p11 document [target]. Use --json format to examine structured comment fields. Post replies using --body-file for multi-line content. Use the name Codex for all replies.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced usage with structured output for large documents

output_examples.input: Review comments on document abc-123 and reply where needed.
output: Reviewed 5 visible threads on document abc-123.
- Thread #4: Asked clarifying question about the proposed API change.
- Thread #7: Confirmed the suggested fix approach and approved.
3 threads had clear decisions and were left for p11:revise.
Please refresh the p11 page to see the replies.

output_examples.input: Check if any threads on my document need replies.
output: All visible threads on document xyz-789 have clear decisions or resolved status.
No replies are needed at this time.
Threads #2 and #5 have settled decisions ready for p11:revise.

output_examples.input: Review document def-456 and reply to unclear threads.
output: Found 3 threads that need replies on document def-456:
- Thread #1: Asked why the timeout value was changed to 30s. Replied with the performance rationale.
- Thread #3: Requested clarification on the error handling approach. Asked which pattern the reviewer prefers.
- Thread #6: Said 'this looks wrong' without detail. Asked for specific concerns.
2 other threads have settled decisions for p11:revise.

best_practices: Provide the p11 read URL or edit URL for the best results and fastest document resolution.
Review the suggested replies before posting to sensitive or high-visibility threads.
Use the skill after p11:revise has processed clear decisions to keep review workflow organized.
anti_patterns: Do not reply to threads that already have clear settled decisions; leave those for p11:revise.
Do not use this skill to modify document content; use p11:revise for content changes.
Do not ask the skill to ignore thread visibility rules or reply to hidden comments.

faq.question: Can this skill reply to hidden or resolved comments?
faq.answer: No. The skill only considers visible threads. Hidden or resolved comments are skipped.

faq.question: What name does the skill use when posting replies?
faq.answer: The skill uses the name Codex for all replies by default.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my document content?
faq.answer: No. This skill only posts replies to comments. Use p11:revise to modify document content.

faq.question: What happens when no threads need replies?
faq.answer: The skill reports that no replies are needed and summarizes any clear decisions found.

faq.question: Can I choose which threads to reply to?
faq.answer: The skill evaluates all visible threads automatically. It decides based on thread content.

faq.question: Does this skill support multi-line replies?
faq.answer: Yes. Use --body-file for multi-line reply content when needed.
